title: Nomad Replica Initialization Replay Exploit
summary: A flawed Replica contract initialization allowed arbitrary messages to be treated as valid, turning the exploit into a public replay drain that extracted roughly $190M before partial recoveries.

classification.privilegeGained: ability to replay arbitrary bridge messages as valid
classification.exploitMechanism: misconfigured initialization caused broad message acceptance

response.summary: Nomad paused bridge activity, coordinated recovery efforts, and publicly tracked whitehat returns, but the bridge failure remained a defining trust breakdown.
actions: worked with ecosystem partners on tracing

attackPath.title: Trusted root initialized incorrectly
attackPath.description: A contract update left the Replica system accepting an invalid root as if it were trusted, weakening message validation.

attackPath.title: First attacker proves arbitrary withdrawal path
attackPath.description: Once one malicious withdrawal succeeded, the calldata pattern became visible and easy for others to imitate.

attackPath.title: Crowd replay drain accelerates losses
attackPath.description: Multiple participants copied and modified successful transactions, rapidly draining assets from the bridge's liquidity.

evidence.claimSupported: Nomad attributed the incident to a Replica authentication failure that let forged messages pass validation and reach the bridge router.
The exploit stemmed from a trust root issue that made copied withdrawals broadly executable.
The incident grew into a mass replay drain with losses around $190M before partial recoveries.
